PETITION RE SUBSTITUTED JUDGMENT TRUST
Off Calendar Jan-12-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jennifer Steneberg for (petitioner) Jesse Guerrero.

The Court granted the petition re substituted judgment trust (subject to review of the order). Status on filing a petition for change of venue is set for January 25, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Henry. (Not Reported)

PETITION RE SUBSTITUTED JUDGMENT TRUST
Off Calendar Nov-30-2015 Continued To Dec-14-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jennifer Steneberg for Petitioner.

The Court inquired regarding the need for a special needs trust. Ms. Steneberg replied that the Conservatee, Linda Guerrero, had sustained an injury which resulted in her need for 24-hour care. She said that estimates for the cost of this care run from $137,000 to $183,000 annually. There is a monthly annuity payment of $8,000, but the annuity is insufficient to cover the costs of the conservatee's care, not to mention her other living expenses. A special needs trust is therefore necessary and appropriate. In response to the Court's inquiry as to why a Wisconsin company was being requested to act as trustee, Ms. Steneberg replied that the company has trust officers in all fifty states, is a leader in administering special needs trusts, charges lower fees than the going rates, and is the preferred choice of the conservatee's family. The Court asked if any California company had been contqcted to serve as trustee, and Ms. Steneberg replied that they had consulted with Wells Fargo, but that its fees were nearly double those of the Wisconsin company. The Court then requested information regarding the nproposed purchase of a vehicle for transporting the conservatee. Ms. Steneberg replied that the conservator was requesting authorizing enpenditure of up to $30,000 for a tall vehicle that the conservatee could easliy get in and out of. The Court requested that a supplemental declaration be provided to the Court more fully addressing the concerns raised at the hearing. Ms. Steneberg stated that she should be able to do that within a week. The matter was continued to December 14, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Lynn O'Malley Taylor. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares.
